scope: DM1 V1 Inventory & Items TODO: Key usage (use key on locked door)
conclusion: No direct DM1 V1 key-on-locked-door behavior is source-backed in the audited ReDMCSB PC click/command/sensor/door route. Do not invent a key-door action; keys remain wall-sensor/object-mechanism behavior unless another primary source proves otherwise.

sourceAnchors.evidence: C080 dungeon viewport clicks dispatch to CLIKVIEW.C:F0377_COMMAND_ProcessType80_ClickInDungeonView.
Front-door click toggles only when the leader hand is empty, DOOR->Button is set, and the door-button/wall-ornament zone is hit; it schedules C10_EVENT_DOOR/C02_EFFECT_TOGGLE.
When the square ahead is a door and the leader hand is not empty, the path attempts thrown-object handling only; no key-type check or door unlock path appears.
Non-empty leader-hand object interaction with wall ornaments/fountains routes only on wall squares, then continues to F0372 front-wall sensor touch.
F0275 click-on-wall sensors compare/use the leader-hand object for C003/C004/C011/C017 and object mechanisms C013/C016; this is wall sensor behavior, not door-square behavior.
Specific-object sensor types are explicitly wall ornament/player-click sensors; event-driven wall sensors are separate.

negativeSearches.result: No direct key-door route found; only unrelated door movement blocker names matched lock/door terms.

verification.command: rg -n -i 'key.*door|door.*key|locked door|unlock.*door|lock.*door|keyhole|key hole' COMMAND.C CLIKVIEW.C MOVESENS.C DUNGEON.C PANEL.C DEFS.H TIMELINE.C DUNVIEW.C
verification.result: PASS only unrelated G0389_B_GroupMovementBlockedByDoor extern matched; no direct key-door route found
